laws.title: Public Law 2-12 | Fee Prohibition Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is no longer operative because a later law expressly repeals H.R.4/PL-2-12 in full; only repeal history survives.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Keep only a historical note for the court-fee prohibition and record the later repeal by PL 4-30 sec. 7.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json classifies PL-002-012 as fully-repealed and ties the repeal to PL 4-30 sec. 7.
usc/usc50.xml:1583 contains rp-pl002012-codification with a repealed/superseded history note.
origin/main has no matching PL-002-012 note in the live XML.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch preserves a history note in usc/usc50.xml, but the proper posture is repeal history only at the Title 28 anchor; origin/main does not carry the note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave PL-002-012 as repeal history only and ensure the repeal marker remains intact.

laws.title: Public Law 2-13 | The Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021
laws.chronology_conclusion: No later law in the reviewed set clearly repeals it, so the 48-hour notice rule remains a live employment-duty statute.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Create a Title 29 employment-notice provision requiring reasons for termination to be given within 48 hours, with a Title 5 cross-reference for federal employees if desired.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json treats the 48-hour termination notice as current Title 29 operative text.
usc/usc05.xml:1999 contains rp-pl002013-codification in the current branch.
origin/main has no matching PL-002-013 note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch parks the law as a note in usc/usc05.xml, and origin/main lacks the note entirely; the operative text still needs a real Title 29 insertion.
laws.final_recommended_action: Codify the termination-notice duty as operative Title 29 text.

laws.title: Public Law 2-14 | National Federal Torts Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The tort statute was later expressly repealed by PL 15-99, so it survives only as repeal history.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Keep a historical note for the federal-torts framework and record the repeal by PL 15-99.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json classifies PL-002-014 as current in the source batch, but later materials supersede it.
usc/usc28.xml:1052 contains rp-pl002014-codification, and the same file also carries rp-pl015099-codification repealing PL 2-14.
origin/main has no matching PL-002-014 note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch holds the old tort note in usc/usc28.xml, but the live XML also includes PL 15-99's repeal of PL 2-14; origin/main does not resolve that history.
laws.final_recommended_action: Treat PL-002-014 as fully repealed and retain only the repeal history.

laws.title: Public Law 3-15 | Admission to the United Nations Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: A later law expressly repeals PL 3-15, so the act is no longer operative.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Keep only a historical note for the UN-admission consent procedure and record the later repeal by PL 15-93.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json maps the admission-consent procedure to a Title 22 anchor.
usc/usc22.xml:60315 contains rp-pl003015-codification.
usc/usc22.xml:60315 also contains rp-pl015093-codification, which expressly repeals PL 3-15.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch still shows the PL-003-015 history note in usc/usc22.xml, but the same file also contains the later repeal note; origin/main lacks the PL-003-015 entry.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave PL-003-015 as repeal history only.

laws.title: Public Law 3-16 | The Capitol Police Establishment
laws.chronology_conclusion: The base Capitol Police statute remains in force, but its deployment section is later rewritten by PL 3-18.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Codify the Capitol Police Board, protective authority, arrest authority, Capitol-complex policing, and the deployment framework, then fold in PL 3-18's Section V rewrite.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json treats PL-003-016 as current but notes later amendment by PL-003-018.
usc/usc18.xml:1326 contains rp-pl003016-codification in the current branch.
PL-003-018 is the same-target amendment law that replaces Section V of PL-003-016.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch keeps the Capitol Police law as a note in usc/usc18.xml, while the same-target amendment is still not integrated into operative Title 2 text; origin/main lacks the branch note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Codify the Title 2 base statute and integrate PL 3-18's replacement Section V.

laws.title: Public Law 3-17 | The Diplomatic Protections Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: A later law expressly repeals PL 3-17 in full, so it survives only as history.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Keep only a historical note for the diplomatic-protection rules and record the later repeal by PL 10-66.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json maps the diplomatic-protection rules to a Title 22 anchor.
usc/usc22.xml:60315 contains rp-pl003017-codification.
usc/usc22.xml also contains rp-pl010066-codification, which expressly repeals PL 3-17.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch still shows the PL-003-017 history note in usc/usc22.xml, but the same file also contains the later repeal note; origin/main lacks the PL-003-017 entry.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave PL-003-017 as repeal history only.

laws.title: Public Law 3-18 | Amendment to S.12
laws.chronology_conclusion: This is a direct amendment to the Capitol Police statute, not a standalone repeal or new framework.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Replace Section V of PL 3-16 in full with the new outside-jurisdiction authority and renamed section title.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json describes PL-003-018 as a direct rewrite of PL-003-016 Section V.
usc/usc50.xml:1583 contains rp-pl003018-codification in the branch.
The base law PL-003-016 is the same-target statute being amended.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch parks PL-003-018 as a Title 50 history note instead of applying the amendment to Title 2; origin/main lacks the branch note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Apply the Section V rewrite directly to the Capitol Police provisions.

laws.title: Public Law 3-19 | The NARA Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The Archivist-notification duty remains operative and has no later repeal in the reviewed materials.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Add the Title 44 duty requiring the President to notify the Archivist of the United States of all bills signed into law.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01.json sends the Archivist-notification duty to Title 44.
usc/usc44.xml:592 contains rp-pl003019-codification in the current branch.
origin/main has no matching PL-003-019 note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch keeps the NARA Act as a Title 44 history note, but origin/main lacks the note; the operative records-duty text still needs to be codified.
laws.final_recommended_action: Codify the Archivist-notification duty as operative Title 44 text.

laws.title: Public Law 3-20 | National Immigration Employment Act of 2021
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source remains unrecoverable, so the act cannot be verified beyond a source-limited placeholder.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Retain only a source-limited historical note until OCR or another extractable text source is recovered; do not invent operative language.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01-03.json marks the source as scanned/unextractable and leaves the law unresolved.
usc/usc08.xml:4406 contains rp-pl003020-codification with an explicit source-limitation note.
origin/main has no matching PL-003-020 note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch note in usc/usc08.xml correctly admits the source limitation, while origin/main lacks the entry; there is still no recoverable operative text.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep PL-003-020 source-limited until the statute text can be recovered.

laws.title: Public Law 3-21 | Effective Judicial Function and General Duties Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The Chief Justice duties and reporting requirements remain operative; no later law in the reviewed set clearly repeals them.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Create a Title 28 section for Chief Justice administrative duties and a companion reporting requirement to Congress and the Judicial Conference.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-01-03.json gives the act as current and locates the operative duties in Title 28.
usc/usc50.xml:1583 contains rp-pl003021-codification in the branch.
origin/main has no matching PL-003-021 note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch still parks PL-003-021 as a Title 50 note, but the operative Title 28 text is absent and origin/main does not supply it either.
laws.final_recommended_action: Codify the Chief Justice duties and reporting rules as actual Title 28 text.
